title: Add an Organize fields by name transformation

blocks.content: The organize fields by name transformation lets you rename, reorder, or hide fields returned by a single query in your panel. This transformation applies only to panels with a single query.
To use this transformation, open an existing panel in edit mode on your dashboard.
Click **Add transformation** and select **Organize fields by name**.

Use the following table as a guideline for the available actions:

| Action | When to use |
| --- | --- |
| Change field order | Reorder fields, manually or automatically. |
| Hide or show a field | Hide fields that aren't relevant. |
| Rename fields | Customize field names. |
Drag fields to reorder them, use the eye icon to hide fields, or type a new name to rename a field.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Transformation editor Organize fields by name']
steps.description: Reorder, hide, or rename fields in this editor, then click anywhere here when you're done.

blocks.content: Click **Save dashboard**, confirm the title, and click **Save**.

The transformation is applied automatically. If it doesn't refresh, click **Refresh** on the panel to verify the visualization looks as intended.

In the next milestone, you'll export your transformed data as a CSV file.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Organize fields by name](/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/transform-data/#organize-fields-by-name)
